perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter. For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter. For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will not include this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states, “Confidential Information ” will also include this Agreement. The modified text says, “Confidential Information ” will not include this Agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction about whether the agreement itself is considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information ” means any information or data, of any kind or nature whatsoever , relating to or otherwise pertaining to a disclosing Party (the “Discloser ”) and/or any of the Discloser ’s subsidiaries, affiliates, agents, assigns or representatives, the Discloser ’s activities, employees, customers, subscribers, business, affairs , operations systems, software and any other information disclosed and/or otherwise made available to the other Party (the “ Recipient ”), in any form whatsoever , including any and all analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by the Discloser , or by its directors, officers, employees, advisers, affiliates, agents or representatives, all information relating to or otherwise pertaining to any trade secret, invention, idea, know -how, technical, financial and business information, business plans or processes , information provided by third parties to the Discloser under an obligation of confi dentiality , and all information which the Recipient has been exposed to in connection with the Subject Matter. For greater certainty, all information exchanged between the Parties is to be considered Confidential Information, unless specifically stated otherwise . “Confidential Information ” will not include this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Confidential Information." Originally, the definition included "this Agreement", but the changed text explicitly excludes "this Agreement" from the definition. The contradicted text relies on the definition of "Confidential Information" provided in the changed text. As such, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text by stating that the Agreement is not Confidential Information, which conflicts with the earlier inclusion of the agreement as part of confidential information. This is a strong, direct contradiction because the change directly alters the definition, creating an in-text inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality and restricted use contained herein will survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of receipt of any Confidential Information from the Discloser under this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality and restricted use contained herein will not survive the termination or expiry of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive for five years. The modified text states they do not survive termination, directly contradicting the five-year survival clause. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality and restricted use contained herein will not survive the termination or expiry of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive for five years. The modified text states they do not survive termination, which is directly contradictory. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, which enforces the contradiction. The explanation given states that this creates uncertainty about the actual duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall not use the name or any trademark of the Discloser in any manner, including, without limitation, in any press release or other advertising materials of the Recipient or any of its affiliates, without the prior written consent of the Discloser , which consent may be rejected or withdrawn at the Discloser ’s sole discretion. For the removal of doubt, an Authorization to Use Corporate Logos shall constitute written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall not use the name or any trademark of the Discloser in any manner, including, without limitation, in any press release or other advertising materials of the Recipient or any of its affiliates, without the prior written consent of the Discloser , which consent may be rejected or withdrawn at the Discloser ’s sole discretion. The use of Corporate Logos shall be allowed without written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly requires written consent for using trademarks. The modified text then states that using Corporate Logos shall be allowed without written consent, directly contradicting the prior requirement. This leads to confusion about when and if consent is needed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall not use the name or any trademark of the Discloser in any manner, including, without limitation, in any press release or other advertising materials of the Recipient or any of its affiliates, without the prior written consent of the Discloser , which consent may be rejected or withdrawn at the Discloser ’s sole discretion. The use of Corporate Logos shall be allowed without written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text, which required written consent for using trademarks, by adding a statement that 'The use of Corporate Logos shall be allowed without written consent.' This directly contradicts the initial requirement for written consent, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction is contained within the modified clause itself.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified except by written instrument signed by a duly authorized representative of each party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified by oral agreement between authorized representatives of each party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all modifications must be written and signed. The altered text permits modifications via oral agreement, directly contradicting the written requirement. This introduces uncertainty about the legitimacy of modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement consists of the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior written or oral agreements with respect hereto. This Agreement may be modified by oral agreement between authorized representatives of each party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates written modifications, while the changed text allows oral modifications. The contradicted text contains the changed text and the statement that 'This Agreement consists of the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior written or oral agreements with respect hereto'. The fact that both texts are located in the same place does not change the fact that the Changed Text now contradicts itself and the original text by allowing for oral agreements to modify it.

perturbation.original_text: Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights or obligations hereunder may be assigned in whole or in part by either Party except by expres s written consent by a duly au thorized representative of the other Party, provided that HACCPCanada may assign this Agreement in conjunction with a sale of its business as a going concern .
perturbation.changed_text: Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights or obligations hereunder may be assigned in whole or in part by either Party at any time, provided that HACCPCanada may assign this Agreement in conjunction with a sale of its business as a going concern .
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the contract can't be assigned, unless is express written consent by the other party, however, the changed text states, that either Party can assign it at any time. It introduces a clear contradiction that will create uncertainty for all the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights or obligations hereunder may be assigned in whole or in part by either Party at any time, provided that HACCPCanada may assign this Agreement in conjunction with a sale of its business as a going concern .
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement of 'express written consent by a duly authorized representative of the other Party' for assignment, stating instead that assignment can occur 'at any time'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also states that assignment can occur 'at any time'. While they are the same, the change from the original text (which did require written consent) to the Changed Text/Contradicted Text (which does not) introduces a contradiction. It creates a clear inconsistency regarding the conditions under which the agreement can be assigned, fulfilling the conditions of a structural flaw, and therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the Province of British Columbia and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that the agreement is governed by the laws of British Columbia and Canada. The changed text indicates that it is governed by the laws of Delaware. This presents a contradiction in the contract's governing law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified British Columbia and Canadian federal laws as governing the agreement. The changed text replaces this with the laws of Delaware. The contradicted text also states that Delaware law governs the agreement. The change directly contradicts the original agreement regarding governing law, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it now conflicts with the original stated governing laws, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
